questions.q: Baji Rao I held which high office in the Maratha Empire?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I served as the Peshwa, or prime minister, of the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: During which years did Baji Rao I serve as Peshwa?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I was Peshwa from 1720 until his death in 1740.

questions.q: Baji Rao I is famously remembered as a general who is said to have done what?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I is celebrated as an undefeated general, said never to have lost a battle.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's rapid campaigns were chiefly built around which arm of war?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I relied on fast-moving light cavalry to strike deep and far across India.

questions.q: Baji Rao I expanded Maratha power chiefly in which direction across the subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I pushed Maratha influence northward across central and northern India.

questions.q: Under which Maratha Chhatrapati did Baji Rao I serve as Peshwa?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I served as Peshwa under Chhatrapati Shahu I, grandson of Shivaji.

questions.q: Baji Rao I belonged to which Brahmin family that became hereditary Peshwas?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I was of the Bhat family, which held the hereditary office of Peshwa.

questions.q: Whom did Baji Rao I succeed as Peshwa in 1720?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I succeeded his father Balaji Vishwanath as Peshwa in 1720.

questions.q: Baji Rao I served during which century CE?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I's career as Peshwa, 1720 to 1740, falls in the 18th century CE.

questions.q: Baji Rao I was the prime minister of which Indian empire?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I was the Peshwa, or prime minister, of the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: At which 1728 battle did Baji Rao I decisively defeat the Nizam of Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I outmanoeuvred and defeated the Nizam at the Battle of Palkhed in 1728.

questions.q: In 1737 Baji Rao I carried out a daring raid right up to the walls of which Mughal capital?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I led a swift raid to the very outskirts of Delhi in 1737, stunning the Mughal court.

questions.q: Baji Rao I again defeated the Nizam at which 1737-38 battle in central India?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I defeated the Nizam a second time near Bhopal, leading to the favourable Convention of Doraha.

questions.q: Which Maratha chief, founder of a major house, rose under Baji Rao I to control Indore and Malwa?
questions.explanation: Malhar Rao Holkar rose under Baji Rao I and founded the Holkar dynasty centred on Indore.

questions.q: Which chief who founded the Scindia house also rose to prominence serving Baji Rao I?
questions.explanation: Ranoji Scindia, an early commander under Baji Rao I, founded the Scindia dynasty of Gwalior.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's younger brother, who served as a key general and revenue manager, was named what?
questions.explanation: Chimaji Appa, Baji Rao I's brother, was a notable general who led the campaign against the Portuguese.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's son and successor as Peshwa was popularly known by what name?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I was succeeded by his son Balaji Baji Rao, popularly called Nana Saheb.

questions.q: Baji Rao I helped establish Maratha dominance over which central Indian region rich in revenue?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I extended firm Maratha control over the fertile region of Malwa in central India.

questions.q: Baji Rao I died in 1740 on the banks of which river while on campaign?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I died at Raverkhedi on the banks of the Narmada in 1740.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's celebrated second wife, remembered in legend, was named what?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I's second wife Mastani, of mixed Rajput-Muslim heritage, became a famous figure in Maratha legend.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's first wife, mother of his successor, was named what?
questions.explanation: Kashibai was Baji Rao I's first wife and the mother of his successor Balaji Baji Rao.

questions.q: The Maratha policy of expanding northward strongly promoted by Baji Rao I is often summed up by which famous saying attributed to him?
options: Strike at the trunk and the branches fall
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I is said to have urged striking at the Mughal trunk so the provincial branches would fall on their own.

questions.q: Approximately how old was Baji Rao I when he was appointed Peshwa in 1720?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I was only about twenty years old when Shahu appointed him Peshwa in 1720.

questions.q: Baji Rao I won the right to collect chauth and sardeshmukhi from which crumbling power across much of India?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I asserted Maratha rights to collect chauth and sardeshmukhi taxes across Mughal-claimed territory.

questions.q: The Battle of Palkhed in 1728 is studied as a classic example of which kind of military operation?
options: A war of movement / strategic manoeuvre
questions.explanation: Palkhed is cited, including by Field Marshal Montgomery, as a masterpiece of strategic mobility and manoeuvre warfare.

questions.q: Baji Rao I supported a Rajput cause by backing which Bundela ruler against Mughal pressure in 1729?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I aided the aged Bundela chief Chhatrasal against the Mughals, gaining territory and an alliance in return.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's chief seat of power and administration was the growing city of what?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I made Pune the effective centre of Peshwa power, developing it as the Maratha administrative capital.

questions.q: Baji Rao I's full given name was what?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I's full name was Baji Rao Ballal Bhat, distinguishing him from later Peshwas of the family.

questions.q: The Maratha campaign that captured Bassein and Salsette from the Portuguese, begun in Baji Rao I's time, was led mainly by whom?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I's brother Chimaji Appa led the Maratha conquest of Bassein and Salsette from the Portuguese around 1739.

questions.q: In recognition of his northern conquests, Baji Rao I is often credited with laying the groundwork for Maratha control over which Mughal heartland region by mid-century?
questions.explanation: Baji Rao I's drives north paved the way for Maratha ascendancy in the Delhi-Agra region in the decades that followed.
